metadata.title: Every valley is a parabola if you lean in close enough
metadata.purpose: vox-explainer slate cut of Quantum Mechanics Vol. 5 candidate 04. Source: M-04 (Series Expansions and Approximation), Every Smooth Potential Near a Minimum Is a Harmonic Oscillator.
metadata.clock: narration (ElevenLabs) — durations below are word-count estimates until GATE 0 audio lock
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL=parabola (the approximation that survives the zoom); CRIMSON=full complicated potential (the thing that looks hard)
metadata.source: Vol. 5, M-04 (Series Expansions and Approximation), Every Smooth Potential Near a Minimum Is a Harmonic Oscillator
metadata.note: Exclusions: do not work the full Taylor coefficients; the zoom is the argument. No full series expansion on screen.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B01: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B12: palette=claude but the outro is 'OutroCTA' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. The quantum harmonic oscillator is everywhere in physics. Molecular bonds, crystal lattices, trapped atoms — all of them. Why does one simple model fit everything?

card.dek: Every valley is a parabola if you lean in close enough

build.suggested: suggested prompt: This is Liam, in for Bear. The quantum harmonic oscillator is everywhere in physics. Molecular bonds, crystal lattices, trapped atoms — all of them. Why does on

beats.narration_text: Nature is full of complicated, lopsided potential wells. A perfect parabola predicts quantum energy levels equally spaced. Real wells are not parabolas. Yet the equally-spaced levels keep showing up. Why?

card.dek: Complicated wells predict complicated spectra. Parabola predicts equal spacing. Real spectra show equal spacing. Why?

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Nature is full of complicated, lopsided potential wells. A perfect parabola predicts quantum energy levels equally spaced. Real wells are not parabolas. Yet the

beats.narration_text: A potential well can have any shape — asymmetric, lopsided, with cusps and bumps. Any smooth function drawn over a minimum.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: A potential well can have any shape — asymmetric, lopsided, with cusps and bumps. Any smooth function drawn over a minimum.

beats.narration_text: Taylor-expand the potential about its minimum. The first derivative is zero — that is what a minimum means. The linear term dies. What is left is a quadratic.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Taylor-expand the potential about its minimum. The first derivative is zero — that is what a minimum means. The linear term dies. What is left is a quadratic.

beats.narration_text: Zoom into the bottom of any smooth well. Watch the well and a fitted parabola approach each other as the region narrows. At the minimum, they merge.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Zoom into the bottom of any smooth well. Watch the well and a fitted parabola approach each other as the region narrows. At the minimum, they merge.

beats.narration_text: The effective spring constant is the second derivative of the potential at the minimum. That is the only number the parabola needs. The shape everywhere else is irrelevant for small oscillations.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The effective spring constant is the second derivative of the potential at the minimum. That is the only number the parabola needs. The shape everywhere else is

beats.narration_text: Every molecular bond, every lattice site, every trapped atom — each has its own complicated potential. But near the bottom, each becomes a parabola with its own spring constant.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Every molecular bond, every lattice site, every trapped atom — each has its own complicated potential. But near the bottom, each becomes a parabola with its own

beats.narration_text: The harmonic oscillator is universal not because nature is simple. It is universal because every minimum lies.

props.headline: The harmonic oscillator is universal not because nature is simple. It…

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The harmonic oscillator is universal not because nature is simple. It is universal because every minimum lies.

beats.narration_text: Illustrative: a Morse potential for a diatomic bond. Far from equilibrium it is asymmetric and anharmonic. Zoom to the bottom: the well and its fitted parabola agree for displacements under 0.05 nanometers.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Illustrative: a Morse potential for a diatomic bond. Far from equilibrium it is asymmetric and anharmonic. Zoom to the bottom: the well and its fitted parabola

beats.narration_text: The linear term dies at any minimum. What remains is a quadratic — a parabola. Zoom close enough and every well becomes harmonic. That is why the harmonic oscillator is everywhere.

card.dek: Linear term = 0 at minimum. Leading survivor = quadratic. Every well is a parabola up close.
